engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 2008 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 2008 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The DV6C BlueHDi brought Euro 6 compliance to the 2008, with SCR and DPF as standard - but introduced AdBlue system complexity.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.6 BlueHDi 100, Allure 1.6 BlueHDi 100, Allure 1.6 BlueHDi 120, GT Line 1.6 BlueHDi 100, GT Line 1.6 BlueHDi 120
commonFailures: DPF blockage on short-journey driving (60k-90k miles)
AdBlue injector failure causing NOx sensor faults
EGR valve carbon buildup leading to reduced power
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6C Peugeot 2008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Higher-output variant of the DV6 family, offering stronger torque for heavier 2008 trims without compromising efficiency.
compatibleVariants: Allure 1.6 BlueHDi 120, GT 1.6 BlueHDi 120, GT Line 1.6 BlueHDi 120
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal leaks at higher mileage
DPF regeneration issues in urban use
EGR cooler clogging after 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6T Peugeot 2008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Pre-BlueHDi HDi unit, simpler emissions setup but less refined; common on early 2008 models before Euro 6.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.6 HDi 92, Allure 1.6 HDi 92, Access 1.6 HDi 92
commonFailures: EGR valve sticking causing rough idle
Injector seal leaks leading to oil dilution
Turbo actuator failure around 70k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HZ Peugeot 2008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: The DV5RC replaced the 1.6L BlueHDi in Gen 2, offering improved efficiency and lower emissions with refined injection.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.5 BlueHDi 100, Allure 1.5 BlueHDi 100, Allure Premium 1.5 BlueHDi 100, GT 1.5 BlueHDi 130, GT Premium 1.5 BlueHDi 130
commonFailures: AdBlue tank sensor faults triggering countdown
DPF pressure sensor errors after city driving
High-pressure fuel pump wear at 90k+ miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5RC Peugeot 2008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level diesel for budget-conscious 2008 buyers; modest power but proven durability in light-duty use.
commonFailures: Turbo wastegate sticking causing boost loss
Glow plug relay failure in cold weather
Oil pump wear leading to low pressure warnings
entries.cta: Get quotes for 8HZ Peugeot 2008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The base PureTech unit that launched the 2008; compact and efficient but later notorious for wet belt degradation issues.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.2 PureTech 82, Active 1.2 PureTech 100, Allure 1.2 PureTech 100, Access 1.2 PureTech 82
commonFailures: Wet timing belt degradation causing oil contamination (40k-70k miles)
Oil pump clogging from belt debris triggering low pressure
Camshaft phaser wear leading to rough running
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2 Peugeot 2008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Turbocharged PureTech variant delivering 130hp; popular in GT trims but shares the wet belt vulnerability of the EB2 family.
compatibleVariants: GT 1.2 PureTech 130, GT Premium 1.2 PureTech 130, GT Line 1.2 PureTech 130, Allure 1.2 PureTech 130
commonFailures: Wet belt failure causing catastrophic oil starvation (50k-80k miles)
Turbo actuator sticking leading to boost loss
High-pressure fuel pump failure causing hard starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2DT Peugeot 2008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-range PureTech with 110hp; introduced revised belt routing but still requires vigilant wet belt maintenance.
compatibleVariants: Allure 1.2 PureTech 110, Allure Premium 1.2 PureTech 110, GT Line 1.2 PureTech 110, Active 1.2 PureTech 110
commonFailures: Timing belt wear accelerating oil contamination
Oil pressure sensor faults giving false warnings
Valve carbon buildup affecting idle quality
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2ADT Peugeot 2008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Early naturally-aspirated VTi variant; robust but underpowered for the 2008's weight, largely replaced by turbo units.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.2 VTi 82, Access 1.2 VTi 82, Allure 1.2 VTi 82
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch causing rattling noise (80k+ miles)
Oil consumption increase due to piston ring wear
Variable valve timing solenoid failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2F Peugeot 2008 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 2008 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot 2008's engine reliability is dominated by the 1.2L PureTech wet belt issue and 1.6L BlueHDi DPF/AdBlue problems. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to weigh repair vs replacement options.

problems.h4: Peugeot 2008 Wet Belt Failure - Why PureTech Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 2008 Active, Allure, GT 1.2 PureTech 82/100/110/130 (2013-2024, EB2/EB2DT/EB2ADT)
problems.rootCause: The wet timing belt runs in oil and degrades over time, shedding rubber particles that clog the oil pump strainer and cause catastrophic oil starvation.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Belt Replacement - Replace wet belt before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain oil, remove sump, replace timing belt kit, flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents engine damage

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Oil System Clean - Address contamination after warning
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full engine flush, oil pump replacement, belt change, new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: May restore function if no bearing damage occurred

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Catastrophic failure route
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded oil pump, new belt, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option once bearings or crankshaft are damaged

problems.recommendation: If your 2008 shows low oil pressure warning but still runs, act immediately - a preventative belt change at a specialist is far cheaper than a full replacement. Once the engine has seized or knocked, only a rebuilt unit makes financial sense.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2 engine prices for your Peugeot 2008

problems.h4: Peugeot 2008 Low Oil Pressure Warning - Immediate Action Required
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 2008 all 1.2 PureTech variants (2013-2024, EB2 family codes)
problems.rootCause: Belt debris blocks the oil pump pickup strainer, starving the engine of lubrication and triggering the dashboard warning.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Oil analysis, sump removal, strainer inspection, system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Only useful if engine hasn't suffered bearing damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After seizure/knocking
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt PureTech with upgraded oil pump and belt
repairOptions.longevity: Required once internal components are damaged

problems.recommendation: The low oil pressure warning on a PureTech 2008 is a critical alert - stop driving immediately. If the engine still turns freely, a specialist clean may save it; if it's knocked or seized, replacement is the only viable route.
problems.cta: Get urgent quotes for Peugeot 2008 PureTech engine replacement

problems.h4: Peugeot 2008 DPF Blockage - Diesel Regeneration Failure
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 2008 Active, Allure, GT 1.6 BlueHDi 100/120 (2013-2019, DV6C/DV6T)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents the DPF from reaching regeneration temperature, causing soot buildup that eventually blocks exhaust flow.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Forced Regeneration - Clear mild blockage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, extended high-speed drive or forced regen cycle
repairOptions.longevity: Works if blockage is early-stage and sensors are functional

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DPF Clean/Replace - Address heavy buildup
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF for off-car cleaning or supply fitted replacement unit
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function if substrate isn't melted or cracked

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - If blockage caused damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt BlueHDi with new DPF, EGR, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Needed if blockage caused turbo or piston damage

problems.recommendation: For a 2008 used mainly for short trips, invest in periodic DPF cleaning before blockage becomes severe. If the DPF has caused turbo damage or excessive backpressure, a rebuilt engine with fresh emissions components offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV6C engine prices for your Peugeot 2008 diesel

problems.h4: Peugeot 2008 AdBlue Injector Failure - Emissions System Fault
problems.rootCause: Crystallised urea deposits clog the AdBlue injector nozzle, triggering fault codes and eventually a countdown to engine immobilisation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injector, ultrasonic clean, system reset, test drive
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before countdown reaches zero

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Injector Replacement - Fit new unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit new AdBlue injector, recalibrate system
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function; may need tank flush if contamination severe

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - If fault caused damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt DV5RC with new emissions hardware, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only if injector failure led to NOx damage or engine derate

problems.recommendation: Address AdBlue warnings immediately - a £300 specialist clean can prevent a £700 injector replacement. If the countdown has started, act fast to avoid immobilisation; replacement engines are only needed if the fault caused secondary damage.
problems.cta: Enquire about DV5RC Peugeot 2008 engine availability

problems.h4: Peugeot 2008 EGR Valve Failure - Loss of Power & Rough Running
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup from exhaust gases sticks the EGR valve open or closed, disrupting air-fuel mixture and triggering fault codes.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Clean - Remove carbon deposits
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve, clean with solvent, reinstall and reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Works if valve mechanism isn't worn or seized

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Replace - Fit new or refurbished unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit new EGR valve, gaskets, recalibrate system
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function; may need intake manifold clean too

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - If failure caused damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel with new EGR, turbo check, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Needed if stuck EGR caused turbo or piston damage

problems.recommendation: EGR issues on the 2008 diesel are common but usually fixable without engine replacement. Clean or replace the valve early to avoid secondary damage; only consider a rebuilt engine if the fault has already caused turbo or internal wear.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 2008 diesel engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 2008
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing PureTech wet belt warnings, BlueHDi DPF blockages, or AdBlue countdowns, EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK specialists who can supply rebuilt engines designed to address these exact failure points. Every unit includes upgraded components, bench testing, and a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - all at prices that make replacing your 2008's engine a smarter choice than scrapping it.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 2008 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 2008 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 2008 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 2008's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The first 2008s launched with 1.2L VTi petrol and 1.6L HDi diesel options; PureTech engines arrived mid-cycle, bringing efficiency but later wet belt concerns.
keyChanges: 2013: Launch with 1.2L VTi 82hp and 1.6L HDi 92hp engines
2014: PureTech 1.2L 100hp introduced, replacing VTi in most trims
knownFor: VTi engines robust but underpowered for crossover weight
Early PureTech units show first wet belt degradation reports
BlueHDi introduction brings AdBlue system complexity
Strong resale value for low-mileage Active and Allure trims

years.preview: Facelift Era: Turbo PureTech & Refined BlueHDi
years.description: The facelifted 2008 introduced turbocharged PureTech 110/130hp and refined BlueHDi units; wet belt awareness grew as failure mileage patterns emerged.
keyChanges: 2017: BlueHDi injection system refined for better DPF regeneration
2018: Final pre-Gen 2 updates; wet belt recall discussions begin
knownFor: Turbo PureTech delivers strong performance but shares wet belt risk
BlueHDi DPF issues more pronounced on urban-use examples
Owner forums report wet belt failures at 50k-70k miles
GT trims command premium for rebuilt engine quotes

years.preview: Gen 2 Launch: Updated PureTech & New 1.5L BlueHDi
years.description: The all-new Gen 2 2008 on CMP platform brought revised PureTech engines and the new 1.5L BlueHDi; wet belt concerns persisted but with improved belt routing.
keyChanges: 2021: Peugeot issues technical bulletin on PureTech belt inspection
knownFor: Revised PureTech belt routing but wet belt degradation still reported
1.5L BlueHDi more efficient but AdBlue injector faults emerge
Gen 2 electronics increase complexity of engine replacement
Strong demand for rebuilt units on high-spec GT Premium trims

years.description: Late Gen 2 2008s feature matured PureTech and BlueHDi units with incremental refinements; replacement demand focuses on high-mileage examples and known failure patterns.
keyChanges: 2022: Minor ECU updates for PureTech to address belt wear monitoring
2023: BlueHDi AdBlue system calibration refined to reduce injector faults
2024: Final production year before potential next-gen model

faq.h2: Peugeot 2008 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 2008 1.2 PureTech engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot 2008 1.2 PureTech engines cost £1,200-£2,100 supply-only in the UK, depending on variant and engine code. Used units start around £600 but carry higher risk.
keyPoints: EB2/EB2DT rebuilt: £1,200-£2,100 typical range
Used PureTech engines: £600-£1,400 with verified history
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 labour depending on location
12-month warranty included on rebuilt units from our network
items.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2 engine prices for your Peugeot 2008

items.question: What causes the Peugeot 2008 1.2 PureTech low oil pressure warning?
items.answer: The low oil pressure warning on Peugeot 2008 PureTech engines is usually caused by wet timing belt degradation clogging the oil pump strainer, starving the engine of lubrication.
keyPoints: Wet belt sheds rubber particles into oil system
Oil pump strainer blocks, triggering dashboard warning
Immediate action required to prevent engine seizure
Rebuilt engines include upgraded oil pump to prevent recurrence
items.cta: Get urgent quotes for Peugeot 2008 PureTech engine replacement

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Peugeot 2008?
items.answer: Yes, replacing a Peugeot 2008 engine is often worthwhile if the vehicle is otherwise sound; rebuilt units cost £1,200-£3,200 versus £6,000+ dealer prices, extending life significantly.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine costs far less than dealer replacement
12-month warranty provides peace of mind post-installation
Consider vehicle value and condition before committing
Supply & fit options simplify the replacement process
items.cta: Find out if your Peugeot 2008 is worth an engine replacement

items.question: What are the most common Peugeot 2008 diesel engine problems?
items.answer: Peugeot 2008 diesel engines commonly suffer DPF blockage on short journeys, AdBlue injector faults on BlueHDi units, and EGR valve carbon buildup causing power loss.
keyPoints: DPF blockage occurs when regeneration cycles are interrupted
AdBlue injector crystallisation triggers countdown to immobilisation
EGR valve sticking causes rough running and fault codes
Rebuilt diesel engines include refreshed emissions components
items.cta: Compare rebuilt DV6C engine prices for your Peugeot 2008 diesel

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 2008 engine replacement take?
items.answer: A Peugeot 2008 engine replacement typically takes 2-3 days from specialist collection to refit, including ECU coding and road testing before return.
keyPoints: Engine removal and prep: 1 day typical
Refit, coding and testing: 1-2 days additional
Supply & fit specialists coordinate collection/delivery
Allow extra time for complex variants or emissions calibration

items.question: What warranty comes with a Peugeot 2008 replacement engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot 2008 engines from EngineMarket's network include a Peugeotmum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at the fitting specialist.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage standard on rebuilt units
Warranty valid at any UK specialist in our network
Used engines may have shorter or supplier-specific coverage
Always confirm exact terms before committing to purchase
items.cta: View Peugeot 2008 engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: Can I fit a Peugeot 2008 engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible, fitting a Peugeot 2008 engine requires specialist tools, ECU coding equipment and emissions system calibration; professional supply & fit is strongly recommended.
keyPoints: Engine removal needs crane, hoist and specialist tools
ECU adaptation reset requires dealer-level diagnostic equipment
Emissions systems need calibration post-installation
Professional fit preserves warranty and ensures compliance
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 2008 engine supply & fit services

items.question: How do I find my Peugeot 2008 engine code?
items.answer: Your Peugeot 2008 engine code is on the V5 registration document under "D.2", stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head, or visible via DVLA lookup using your registration.
keyPoints: Check V5 logbook section D.2 for engine code
Code stamped on engine block (EB2, DV6C etc.)
Use our registration lookup for instant verification
Correct code essential for ordering compatible replacement
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot 2008 engine code instantly

items.question: Are Peugeot 2008 petrol or diesel engines more reliable?
items.answer: Diesel Peugeot 2008 engines (1.6L BlueHDi) generally show better long-term reliability than petrol PureTech units, which are prone to wet belt degradation requiring vigilant maintenance.
keyPoints: BlueHDi diesels robust with proper DPF/AdBlue care
PureTech petrol engines require strict belt maintenance
Diesel better for high-mileage, motorway-heavy use
Petrol suits low-mileage urban driving with belt checks
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 2008 petrol vs diesel engine options
